calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar May-01-2020 Vacated

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jun-12-2019 Set For Trial On May-04-20 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Motion To Sever And Motion To Change Venue; Memorandum In Support; And Declaration Of Jose L. Reynoso
Off Calendar May-29-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Nikoloaus Reed, Esq. 415-940-7766 of the Law office of Nikolaus Reed for Plaintiff.
No other Appearance.

RULING - The matter is Uncontested. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT JENNY LEE, SONG SOONYI's Motion To Sever And Motion To Change Venue.

Defendants Jenny Lee and Song Soonyi's motions to change venue and to sever are denied. Venue is proper in this court because the action was filed in the county where one of the defendants resides. (Code Civ. Proc. 395(a).) Moving defendants have not shown that Plaintiff improperly joined the resident defendant. "A defendant's residence for venue purposes may only be disregarded when the joinder is sham, against one having no real interest in the litigation, who is joined solely for the purpose of achieving venue in a particular county." (Buran Equipment Co. v. Superior Court (1987) 190 Cal.App.3d 1662, 1666.) Nor have they shown that the San Francisco resident co-defendant was not joined in good faith, as the complaint states a cause of action against him. (See Gutierrez v. Superior Court (1966) 243 Cal.App.2d 710, 725-26 ["the usual test by which to determine whether a defendant has been joined in good faith is whether or not the complaint states a cause of action against him."].) To the extent that Defendants' argument is based on misjoinder under Code of Civil Procedure 379(c), it is ill-founded. "[A] plaintiff may join two independent and successive tortfeasors as defendants in a single action if he pleads facts showing that he entertains a reasonable doubt as to which defendant is liable for his injuries, or the extent to which each may be liable." (Landau v. Salam (1971) 4 Cal.3d 901, 903 [plaintiff, who claimed to have been injured as the result of both the negligent driving of an automobile and the negligent maintenance of property on a later date, properly joined defendants responsible for both incidents].)

Plaintiff's complaint is not a "mixed action" because the same venue provision, section 395(a) of the Code of Civil Procedure, applies to plaintiff's motor vehicle and general negligence causes of action against all defendants. (Cf. Brown v. Superior Court (1984) 37 Cal.3d 477.)

Defendants have failed to show that separate trials are warranted under Code of Civil Procedure 1048. Any inconvenience of witnesses does not outweigh the risk of inconsistent rulings as to the extent each accident caused plaintiff's injuries and the duplication of effort in presenting evidence related to the medical treatment of these injuries and Plaintiff's alleged damages.

Defendants' motion to transfer venue on the basis of inconvenience to witnesses and promotion of the ends of justice under Code of Civil Procedure section 397(c) is untimely because moving defendants have not yet filed an answer to the complaint. Such a motion cannot be made before an answer is filed. (Buran Equipment Co., 190 Cal.App.3d at 1665; Delgado v. Superior Court (1977) 74 Cal.App.3d 560, 562-563.)

Plaintiff to submit a proposed order that is verbatim with the Court's ruling.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/EPS)
